message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "claim references item type not in policy → throws"
Current error: expected function to throw, but it doesn't — handleClaim looks up item by itemType, returns undefined silently
Passing tests so far: 27

The test (around line 470-487 in spec) quotes a single sword, then submits a claim whose damages reference itemType "amulet". The current handleClaim uses `items.find((i) => i.type === damage.itemType)` and silently treats missing items as if no special clause applies. It must throw instead.

Implement the minimal code change in src/claim-office.ts so this test passes. Specifically, in computeDamagePayout (or wherever damages are processed), throw an Error when the damaged itemType doesn't match any item in the policy's items list.

Do NOT refactor — that comes in the next phase. Run `pnpm test` to verify all 28 tests pass.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal change needed.
Now I understand. I need to make `computeDamagePayout` throw when the item type isn't in the policy. The minimal change is to throw when `item` is undefined.

content.text:
All 28 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added a guard in `computeDamagePayout` that throws `Error("Damaged item type not in policy: ...")` when no policy item matches `damage.itemType`.
- Result: All 28 tests now pass (2 todo remain).
- Approach: Minimal three-line addition — guards the missing-item case immediately after the existing `find`, leaves all other logic untouched. Since the guard ensures `item` is defined past that point, the redundant `item &&` in the ternary was removed (one-token simplification forced by the guard).

Relevant file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-57-53_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
